title: How to setup your Steering Wheel

messages.content: # Recently bought Assetto Corsa or recently got a new wheel? Follow this guide!

# Step 1:
> ### Download your wheel's firmware/ drivers:
> Search: `(company) (wheel name) wheel firmware` 
> Example: "Moza R12 wheel firmware"
> -# (If you have a Logitech, GHub must stay open at all times during use

# Step 2:
> ### Calibrate and setup your wheel in that firmware
> **Disclaimer:** Every wheel's software is different. If needed, reach out to the company's support page to receive help with this step.
> 
> <#1322222599098339359>

# Step 3:
> ### Setup axis's in Content Manager
> 1. Open Content Manager and go to Settings > Assetto Corsa > Controls > Input Method: Wheel > Axis
> 
> 2. Bind the axis's -> Throttle/ Brake/ Clutch/ Handbrake
> 
> 3. Set your Steering Degrees to match your Steering Wheel’s
## 🚨  Warning: If you have a bright red line under the axis, you will need to invert your axis. 🚨

# Step 4
> ### Bind your shifters
> Content Manager -> Settings -> Assetto Corsa -> Controls -> Buttons
> 
> **Paddle Shifters:**
> Click on buttons and bind “Next Gear” and “Previous Gear” to your paddle shifters
> 
> **H-Shifter:**
> Check “Use H-Shifter” and bind the buttons.
> -# Not all cars are H shifter compatible. Join a server and hover over the car, if it shows the manual tag, then you can use your H shifter.

### Useful Setup Options:
> Teleport back to pits: 
> <#1322252040117948556>
> Teleport to other pits: 
> <#1322256613595283536>
